html: 1 &oge; … &oge; fL, with no merging or branching (see section 14.7 for how to handle more complicated graphs with merge and branch operations). Input: parameter vector θ = {θl}L , f1, …, fL, f'1, …, f'L, training datapoint {x0, y} Loss function ℒ: ℝN → ℝ Output: gradient direction ∂J/∂θ = {∂J/∂θl}L l=1 Forward pass: 5   for l = 1, ..., L do 6     xl = fl(xl-1, θl) Backward pass: 8   gL = ℒ'(xL, y) 9   for l = L, ..., 1 do 11     Ll = f'l(xl-1, θl) 12     gl-1 = glLlx 13     ∂J/∂θl = glLlθ
